text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 25 October 2024

Dr Fiona
Centre for Epilepsy
Queen Square, London WC1N 3BG

Dear Dr Fiona,

Amelia Hart, DOB: 22-11-1995, Hospital No: H3829471 NHS No. 9472635180
Flat 3, 28 Torrington Place, London, WC1E 7HX

Generalised Epilepsy

Medication: Ongoing Levetiracetam commenced March 2024; Clobazam as rescue per prior plan

Current Doses:
- Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily (self-reported adherence >95%)
- Clobazam 10 mg at night taken intermittently for clusters (none required in the last 6 weeks)

Therapeutic Monitoring:
- Levetiracetam trough level (ED draw) 11 July 2024: 17 mg/L (laboratory reference 12–46 mg/L). Dose at time of sampling: 1000 mg twice daily

Seizures:
- She told me that since her last clinic appointment she has had 2 - 3 myoclonic jerks, the last reported on 24 August. These were brief, morning events without loss of awareness, no injury, and no progression to generalised tonic–clonic seizure. No auras or focal features described.

History Since Last Review:
- Multiple recent ED triage records exist; nursing documentation summarised where relevant. On 11 July she attended for transient palpitations and light-headedness after poor sleep; no seizure was witnessed, neurological examination was normal, capillary glucose 5.3 mmol/L, vitals stable; discharged with reassurance. On 25 August she presented for advice after a single morning myoclonic jerk; no further events in ED, no head injury, and she self-discharged after observation.
- She denies missed doses in the week preceding the 24 August jerk but reports two nights of curtailed sleep and increased caffeine intake due to shift work.

Epilepsy Classification:
- The clinical picture, history of morning myoclonus, normal MRI (April 2023), and prior EEG with generalised 3–4 Hz spike–wave discharges (February 2024) are consistent with generalised epilepsy. We have discussed typical precipitants including sleep deprivation, stress, and alcohol.

Plan and Discussion:
- Continue Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily. No medication changes made today given low myoclonic burden and absence of generalised tonic–clonic seizures since January 2024.
- Reinforced sleep hygiene and avoidance of excess caffeine; patient identifies shift pattern changes in November and will update us if mornings become more difficult.
- Clobazam to remain available for short-lived clusters only; she has not required it in the last 6 weeks.
- Will arrange repeat EEG if myoclonic frequency increases or if any generalised convulsive seizures recur.
- Safety advice reiterated: driving regulations reviewed; she currently meets DVLA guidance given no loss of awareness events since January and only isolated myoclonic jerks without functional impairment; she understands to inform us immediately if this changes.
- Follow-up: telephone review in 4 months or sooner via advice line if escalation occurs.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: she has had 2 - 3 myoclonic jerks, the last reported on 24 August
She told me that since her last clinic appointment she has had 2 - 3 myoclonic jerks, the last reported on 24 August

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter reports seizures since the last clinic appointment: 'she has had 2 - 3 myoclonic jerks, the last reported on 24 August.' The clinic date is 25 October 2024. The time window 'since her last clinic appointment' is unspecified, so we cannot calculate a rate per defined time unit. There is not enough information to express frequency per day/week/month/year, nor to confirm seizure freedom for ≥6 months (last event 24 August, which is about 2 months prior). Therefore, the precise frequency cannot be determined.
